heroHeadline: Hamas Misses Second BoP Deadline; Signals Police Weapons Handover — Excluding Military Arsenal
heroSubtitle: The deadliest attack on Jewish people since the Holocaust — assault timeline, hostage crisis resolved Jan 2026, intelligence failures, and IDF response. Hamas missed a second Board of Peace disarmament deadline on Apr 19 and proposed handing over only police weapons while explicitly excluding the Qassam Brigades' military arsenal. The BoP's eight-month disarmament plan — requiring Hamas to surrender rockets, drones, and tunnel maps within 90 days — was published in full on Apr 20. Hamas countered with a three-year alternative. Cairo backchannel talks continue despite the stalemate. IDF strikes killed six Palestinians including two children on Apr 15. Six months into the ceasefire, less than $1B of $17B pledged reconstruction funds received.
footerNote: Data sourced from IDF, Israel Police, UN OCHA, Israeli government, Reuters, AP, Haaretz, and institutional analyses. Casualty figures are official Israeli counts; Hamas-theater figures are separately noted and contested. All 251 hostages accounted for as of January 26, 2026. Cumulative conflict dead (Oct 7, 2023 – Mar 25, 2026): 72,265 Palestinians per OCHA/Gaza MoH. Ceasefire in effect since Oct 2025; 2,400+ violations documented by Gaza GMO as of Apr 17, 2026.
